channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Where's the raccoon with the ham radio? (ChatGPT Images 2.0)
item.description: Simon Willison tested ChatGPT Images 2.0 with a Where's-Waldo-style prompt and compares output against gpt-image-1. Sam Altman claimed on the livestream that the leap was equivalent to jumping from GPT-3 to GPT-5; Simon's review adds nuance from real prompts.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Images 2.0 with Instant and Thinking variants
item.description: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 globally with Instant and Thinking variants that can generate up to 2K-resolution images, render non-Latin scripts (Japanese, Korean, Hindi, Bengali) more accurately, and optionally search the web while composing an image. A more powerful tier is reserved for paying subscribers.

item.title: Three new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
item.description: Google announced three new agentic safety and policy features integrated into Ads Advisor, designed to protect advertiser accounts and streamline campaign creation. The update adds real-time policy checks and automated anomaly detection for generated ads.

item.title: Chollet: current AI systems lack metacognition — they cannot accurately model their own capabilities
item.description: François Chollet argues that LLMs fundamentally lack metacognition: they cannot accurately assess what they know or don't know, producing confident errors and unreliable self-evaluation. He frames this as a key gap separating current systems from robust general reasoning, with direct implications for building reliable agentic pipelines.

item.title: QIMMA: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
item.description: Hugging Face and TII launch QIMMA, a quality-first leaderboard for Arabic LLMs covering dialect, culture, and reasoning benchmarks. Fills a critical gap in non-English model evaluation, relevant for any multilingual or regional AI deployment work.

item.title: LeCun: AI existential risk framing is wrong — misuse and power concentration are the real dangers
item.description: Yann LeCun pushes back on p(doom) framing, arguing the real AI risk is not autonomous superintelligence but concentration of AI power in a few companies and misuse by state or corporate actors. He calls for open-source AI development and international governance structures as the appropriate countermeasures.

item.title: OpenAI Images v2 rolling out to all ChatGPT accounts
item.description: OpenAI Images v2, an upgraded image generation capability, is now available across all ChatGPT tiers. Confirmed via Logan Kilpatrick on X.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, hiring senior designers at $400K+
item.description: Anthropic announces Claude Design and is hiring senior designers at $400K+ compensation. Signals a design-first product direction for Claude alongside the engineering-centric Claude Code push.

item.title: When AI quietly shifts what organisations value
item.description: Tech New Zealand examines how generative AI embedded in education practice can gradually reshape institutional values — a "value drift" similar to how social media quietly rewrote norms around privacy. Relevant for Aotearoa organisations adopting AI without explicit policy guardrails.

item.title: Anthropic says OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage is allowed again
item.description: Anthropic has updated its provider documentation to permit OpenClaw-style Claude CLI integrations after a prior restriction. The move re-opens a popular path for local agent tooling that had been in limbo for two weeks. HN front page with 465 points.

item.title: Google: Gemini expands in Chrome to more Asia-Pacific countries with summarization and image editing
item.description: Google announced Gemini features in Chrome are expanding to more Asia-Pacific countries, adding page summarization, image editing, and broader integrations. The rollout extends Google's browser-native AI layer to a wider international audience as part of Chrome's AI-first roadmap.

item.title: Jeff Bezos' Project Prometheus nears $10B fundraising deal at $38B valuation
item.description: Jeff Bezos' AI industrial venture Project Prometheus is nearing completion of a $10 billion fundraising round at a $38 billion post-money valuation, following an initial $6.2 billion raise in November 2025. The company focuses on applying AI to industrial applications, positioning it as a major new frontier AI player alongside established labs.

item.title: Anthropic board director Chris Liddell: NZ has 5 years — not 20 — to adapt to AI
item.description: Chris Liddell, Anthropic board director and NEXT Foundation chairman, warned New Zealand it has 5 years — not 20 — to adapt or risk falling behind in the AI era. His comments during Microsoft's Auckland AI summit frame AI readiness as a national emergency.

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
item.description: Guide on building Korean AI agents using NVIDIA Nemotron with synthetic personas to ensure demographic accuracy and cultural relevance in AI systems.

item.title: Moonshot Kimi K2.6: world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6
item.description: Moonshot released Kimi K2.6, a 1T-parameter MoE open model with 32B active parameters, achieving strong performance on coding and agent benchmarks. Capabilities in long-horizon execution and multi-agent coordination.

item.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
item.description: HuggingFace argues that open AI models are essential for cybersecurity: open weights let defenders audit, adapt, and harden AI systems without vendor lock-in, while closed models create opaque attack surfaces. The post surveys open-source security tooling built on HF-hosted models and calls for the community to prioritise security benchmarks alongside capability evals.

item.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
item.description: OpenAI launches Codex Labs for enterprise, partnering with Accenture, PwC, and Infosys to deploy Codex across software development lifecycles at scale. The rollout comes as Codex reached 4 million weekly active users in under two weeks, validating rapid adoption of AI-native coding agents beyond individual developers.

item.title: Microsoft doubles AI upskilling commitment in NZ, targeting 200,000 Kiwis by 2028
item.description: Microsoft President Jane Livesey announced NZ$9.4 billion in economic contribution to New Zealand in FY25 and a commitment to double AI and digital upskilling initiatives, reaching 200,000 Kiwis by 2028. The report highlights the NZ North datacentre region's role in enabling digital sovereignty alongside Microsoft's AI partnership commitments.

item.title: Microsoft CEO in Auckland: AI could add $102B to NZ economy by 2038
item.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella visited Auckland and projected AI could add NZ$102 billion to New Zealand's economy by 2038. Microsoft also announced expanded AI training initiatives alongside the visit, framing AI adoption as urgent for NZ productivity and competitiveness.

item.title: NZ Law Commission launches review of AI in public sector decision-making
item.description: New Zealand's Law Commission is launching a formal review of AI use in public sector decision-making, responding to legal challenges around AI-driven government outcomes. The review will examine accountability, transparency, and citizen appeal rights for automated decisions.

item.title: NZ startup Ideally raises $16M at $100M valuation for AI market research platform
item.description: Auckland-based Ideally raised NZ$16M at a NZ$100M valuation for its AI-powered market research platform. The tool automates consumer insights gathering for enterprise clients, compressing research cycles from weeks to hours.

item.title: Christchurch-developed AI tool improves wildfire prediction accuracy by 30%
item.description: A machine learning forecasting tool developed in Christchurch achieved 30% improvement in wildfire prediction accuracy using historical fire data from Australia. The model uses ensemble methods and is being evaluated for deployment across Pacific fire-prone regions.

item.title: Canva CEO on building proprietary AI, token pricing, and competing with AI-first design tools
item.description: Canva CEO Melanie Perkins discusses the company's enterprise expansion, its investment in proprietary AI models, and how token-based pricing is reshaping competitive dynamics in the design software market. She addresses Canva's positioning against Adobe's AI suite and the growing wave of AI-first design entrants.

item.title: Maximizing Memory Efficiency to Run Bigger Models on NVIDIA Jetson
item.description: Technical guide covering quantization, flash attention, and KV cache management techniques to deploy multi-billion-parameter generative AI models on memory-constrained NVIDIA Jetson edge devices.

item.title: Run High-Throughput Reinforcement Learning Training with End-to-End FP8 Precision
item.description: NVIDIA details end-to-end FP8 precision training for LLM reinforcement learning using GRPO, addressing GPU utilization bottlenecks during high-intensity RL phases with significant throughput improvements.

item.title: Karpathy: Bryan Johnson uses Claude to analyze 1.5 billion personal health data points
item.description: Andrej Karpathy highlights Bryan Johnson's deep-dive into personal health analytics using Claude across 1.5 billion data points. Demonstrates frontier use of LLMs for personal longitudinal data reasoning at extreme scale.

item.title: AI told NZ tenant to claim $40,000; Tenancy Tribunal awarded $80
item.description: A New Zealand tenant relied on an AI assistant to calculate her rental bond dispute — the AI suggested a $40,000 claim; the Tenancy Tribunal awarded $80. The case is circulating as a cautionary example of LLM hallucination risk in legal contexts.

item.title: Leaked deck reveals OpenAI ad partner selling ChatGPT placements by prompt relevance
item.description: StackAdapt, an OpenAI advertising partner, is selling ChatGPT ad placements targeted by user query relevance according to a leaked internal deck. This marks a new monetization phase for LLM chat interfaces through contextual advertising.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out Chronicle for Codex on macOS
item.description: OpenAI introduced Chronicle, a feature that builds memories from screen captures to enhance Codex's contextual awareness. Currently a research preview for Pro subscribers on macOS.

item.title: Amazon invests $25B more in Anthropic, total now $33B
item.description: Amazon committed an additional $25 billion investment in Anthropic, bringing total investment to $33 billion. Anthropic pledged to spend over $100 billion on AWS infrastructure over the next decade.
